ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for user authentication */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "health_indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health indicator */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient */,
  "indicator_name" TEXT /* Name of the health indicator (e.g., blood pressure, BMI) */,
  "indicator_value" TEXT /* Value of the health indicator */,
  "indicator_date" TEXT /* Date when the health indicator was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient whose data was accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the patient data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the patient data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
column_names: ID of the role assigned to the user
Unique identifier for each health indicator
Name of the health indicator (e.g., blood pressure, BMI)
Date when the health indicator was recorded
Unique identifier for each access event
ID of the patient whose data was accessed
ID of the user accessing the patient data
Date when the patient data was accessed
